en.title: AI Conversation
en.prompt: In the following scenario, someone said something to me. Please help me analyze what the other person might want to express and provide a suitable response. Scenario: [Describe a specific situation]. The speaker says: [Specific words]. What could be the other person's intention? How should I respond?
en.remark: 空気を読む read the air. For some incomprehensible conversations, provide the context of the conversation for AI to interpret and formulate an appropriate response.

related.title: Succubus
related.remark: ⚠️Before using this cue, it is necessary to unlock the developer mode, allowing the AI to portray a seductive character, which is particularly suitable for intimate plotlines in literature. Contributed by @mrdog233o5.

related.title: Opposite-sex Dialogue
related.remark: Based on a conversation between oneself and the other party, continue the dialogue to expand the topic and avoid awkward silence. The prompt words need to be modified according to one's own situation. Contributed by @lsdt45.

related.title: AI Psychotherapy Experience
related.remark: Guiding AI counselors to fully embody the role of a psychotherapy expert, providing you with a thorough and comprehensive psychotherapeutic experience. Contributed by @Antoine2033.

metaDescription: Confused by a comment? Analyze hidden meanings and intentions. Get advice on how to respond appropriately in social situations.

faq.q: Is the AI's reading of "subtext" reliable?
faq.a: Eastern and Western contexts differ significantly. The AI sometimes applies a Western "indirect communication" framework to East Asian scenes, potentially over-reading or missing cues. State the cultural context (East Asian workplace, family, romance) and the AI gets closer to reality.

faq.q: Does it handle passive aggression and insinuation?
faq.a: It recognizes typical passive aggression (like "wow, you're so impressive"), but subtle sarcasm (especially involving local memes) often slips past. For uncertain readings, follow up with "what does this typically mean in simplified Chinese internet context," for sharper interpretation.
